title: Chapter 7 Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of data structures!

questions.question: Lists are mutable (can be changed).
questions.explanation: Lists are mutable — you can add, remove, and modify items.

questions.question: What is [1, 2, 3][−1]?
questions.explanation: Negative index -1 gets the last element: 3

questions.question: The _____ method adds an item to the end of a list.
questions.explanation: append() adds a single item to the end of a list.

questions.question: What makes tuples different from lists?
questions.explanation: The key difference is immutability — tuples cannot be changed.

questions.question: Dictionaries use what to access values?
questions.explanation: Dictionaries use keys to access values: dict["key"]

questions.question: Sets can contain duplicate items.
questions.explanation: Sets automatically remove duplicates — each item appears only once.

questions.question: What does get() return if key doesn't exist?
questions.explanation: get() returns None (or your specified default) instead of an error.

questions.question: Use _____ to create an empty set (not {}).
questions.explanation: {} creates an empty dict! Use set() for an empty set.

questions.question: What does list[::-1] do?
questions.explanation: [::-1] creates a reversed copy of the list.

questions.question: What does set intersection (&) return?
questions.explanation: Intersection returns only items that appear in both sets.

questions.question: Tuple unpacking: a, b = (1, 2) assigns 1 to a and 2 to b.
questions.explanation: Tuple unpacking assigns each element to a corresponding variable.
